title: AuthRequired::None cap-badge inversion
body: The affordances panel shows the `grant` message with required=None yet authorized=false, and firing it is refused with 'does not satisfy None'. The cap badge is is_attenuation(held, req) = req.is_narrower_or_equal(held); the lattice models None as the TOP (widest), so a None-required affordance (documented 'always allowed') reports unauthorized for every holder whose rights are not exactly None. Correct for the middle tiers, inverted for None.
repro: affordances user → grant shows required=None/authorized=false; act user grant → refused by the cap-gate

title: Cell census 4 vs 8 — reflexive UI cells (resolved, not a bug)
body: The cockpit header shows 8 cells; the raw demo_world ledger has 4. Cockpit::with_node installs reflexive UI-scaffolding cells (buffer/inspector-view/workspace backings) on top of demo_world's 4 protocol cells. The atlas crawls the 4-cell protocol substrate; the +4 are documented in the UI atlas.
repro: dregg-mcp survey → 4 cells; cockpit GRAPH tab → 8 cells

title: The issuer well (negative balance) cannot initiate any turn
body: In the game-tree crawl, every turn AUTHORED by the issuer-well cell (3cc026, balance -1000000) is refused with InsufficientBalance — including peek (EmitEvent) and touch (IncrementNonce), which mutate no value. The well cannot pay the per-turn computron fee from a negative balance, so it can never be a turn's agent. This is arguably correct (an issuer well is a passive supply sink that only moves when OTHER cells transact against it), but it means a fee-charged turn is gated on positive balance even for value-neutral verbs. Surfaced by the crawl, not previously noted.
repro: effect from=service(the well) kind=transfer → refused; act on the well → InsufficientBalance
